title: AML Sanctions Evasion Network Investigation and SAR Decision Pack
expected_economic_value: $150k-$275k senior AML sanctions investigation / financial crime compliance role
normalized_title: AML Sanctions Evasion Network Investigation and SAR Decision Pack
instruction: A bank financial-intelligence unit must decide whether to file a SAR, reject pending wires, and exit a customer relationship after a synthetic trade-finance customer shows sanctions-evasion typologies across shell-company payments and crypto off-ramps. Case packet uses fully synthetic names. Customer C-771 Atlas Meridian Import LLC, onboarded 2024-11-02, expected activity=$1.2M/month electronics imports from Singapore and Germany, stated beneficial owners: Mira Chen 55%, Daniel Rios 45%, risk rating at onboarding=medium. Transactions last 45 days: T1 $486,200 to North Caspian Components FZE, UAE, invoice INV-441 servers, goods weight=18 kg, expected comparable customs value=$92,000; T2 $512,900 to Baltic Relay OU, Estonia, invoice chips, beneficiary bank changed 2 hours before wire; T3 $79,500 incoming from Clearwell Robotics Ltd, UK, no invoice; T4 $244,000 to Volga Transit DMCC, UAE, payment note freight equalization, vessel field blank; T5 $98,600 to crypto_exchange deposit wallet W-9A with memo consulting; T6 $501,100 to North Caspian Components FZE split into 5 wires under $105,000 over 2 days; T7 $38,800 incoming from private-wallet OTC broker, originator address missing. Screening excerpt: sanctions_entry S1 North Caspian Machinery LLC jurisdiction=RU program=EO14024 aliases=[North Caspian Mach, N Caspian Machinery] match_score_to_beneficiary=86; S2 Volga Freight JSC jurisdiction=RU aliases=[Volga Transit Logistics] match_score_to_beneficiary=72; PEP_entry P1 Daniil Riosov state procurement deputy DOB_year=1978 match_to_Daniel_Rios=64. KYC anomalies: beneficial-owner passport for Rios expired 2025-12-01; website domain age=38 days; shipping documents reuse container_id MSKU-773102 across T1 and T4; customer explanation says urgent GPU shortage, but HS code listed=8542.31 microcontrollers. Rules and constraints: risk score = sanctions_near_match>=85 add 35, match 70-84 add 18, trade_overvaluation_ratio>3 add 25, split_payments add 16, crypto_offramp add 14, expired_BO_document add 10, impossible_or_reused_shipping add 18, inconsistent_HS_goods add 12, PEP_match>=60 add 8; SAR recommended if score>=70 or any sanctions_near_match>=85 with suspicious trade rationale; reject pending wires if OFAC escalation required or beneficiary match>=85 until sanctions officer clears; do not call any synthetic party a true sanctioned person, classify as potential match requiring escalation. Required work: deliver aml_sanctions_investigation.xlsx + sar_decision_narrative.md with transaction graph, red-flag scoring, sanctions-escalation queue, evidence table, filing/reject/exit recommendation, and a concise SAR-style chronology grounded only in the case packet. This task is self-contained: use the task packet as the factual record, do not add source references or external datasets, and preserve a high-fidelity audit trail for every calculation, classification, and recommendation. State assumptions and unresolved evidence gaps explicitly. When executed locally, place final deliverables under the artifacts/ directory.
allowed_tools: Local text editor or Markdown editor
Local spreadsheet, CSV, JSON, YAML, or scripting tools for deterministic calculations
Local plotting or diagramming tools when useful
disallowed_tools: External fact lookup or live data pulls
Live production systems, cloud accounts, trading systems, clinical systems, or industrial controls
Secret discovery, credential testing, or any action outside the prepared task workspace
input_requirements: Use the task packet as the sole factual input.
Cite row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, and option IDs from the packet wherever they control a conclusion.
Mark missing, contradictory, or under-supported values as needs_review instead of inventing them.
inputs: All factual inputs are embedded in the task packet.
Use row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, option IDs, and deliverable names exactly as provided.
output_requirements: Produce the required deliverable files with the specified filenames when filenames are provided.
Include intermediate calculations or executable logic sufficient for another reviewer to reproduce the result.
Include validation checks, sensitivity notes around controlling thresholds, and a final decision or recommendation table.
constraints: Use the task packet as the factual record.
Do not browse, call live services, import external datasets, or invent missing values.
Treat regulated or safety-critical outputs as review packets, not authorization to act.
quality_bar: Treat the task as realistic professional work with domain-appropriate rigor.
Preserve the packet's domain detail, identifiers, thresholds, and edge cases in the final deliverables.
Make every recommendation traceable to explicit task-packet evidence or mark it as needs_review.
validation_expectations: Check required fields, row counts, totals, thresholds, and classifications before finalizing.
Show enough formulas, script logic, or tables for an independent reviewer to reproduce the result.
Flag any contradiction, missing input, or threshold-sensitive conclusion.
edge_cases: If packet evidence is incomplete, do not infer missing values; label the item needs_review.
If multiple options satisfy hard constraints, rank them using the packet's stated priorities and explain ties.
If a regulated or safety-critical conclusion appears, frame the output as a review packet rather than authorization to act.
privacy_safety_expectations: Do not use live systems, private accounts, credentials, or external datasets.
Do not include raw secrets or operational instructions that would execute real-world changes.
Keep synthetic identifiers from the packet only when needed for verification.
